title: hedyotis purpurea var. floridana
intro: Hedyotis purpurea var. floridana, also known as the Florida pennywort or wild quinine, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of malaria, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antibacterial properties. Hedyotis purpurea var. floridana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
